title: Halogen SPARK!
text:
Halogen SPARK! - SupportGoWhere
Halogen SPARK!
Share link
A structured evidence-based entrepreneurship curriculum that targets Secondary 3 students who are from the G1/G2 Subject Based Banding (SBB) Stream that improves better school-focused outcomes, including their attendance and academic performance.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS

About this support

Halogen SPARK! is an upstream intervention programme that targets Secondary students who are from the G1/G2 SBB stream. It targets pre/low/mid-risk students from the Financial Assistance Scheme (FAS) and/or come from challenging and disadvantaged backgrounds.

The programme is a structured evidence-based programme developed by NFTE (Network for Teaching Entrepreneurship), where the key objectives are to increase service users' social-emotional and academic competencies through the incorporation of English and Math in the programme curriculum. It supports the development of numeracy and literacy skills through the entrepreneurship modules, and aims to instill self-confidence and self-efficacy in youths.

Who is this for?

Secondary 3 students who are from the G1/G2 Subject Based Banding (SBB) stream

What to expect?

The one year programme is available in selected partner schools.

Halogen SPARK! will conduct a pre-programme survey for all enrolled students for the purpose of further adjustments to the programmes depending on the student demographics.

How to apply?

Schools will refer the students to the programme at the start of the calendar year.

Halogen SPARK! and teachers will jointly decide on the identified students on their service eligibility.

Information last updated on 22 Sep 2025
